text: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. Pack my box with five dozen liquor jugs. How vexingly quick daft zebras jump! The five boxing wizards jump quickly. Sphinx of black quartz, judge my vow.

Byte-level text processing allows models to handle any language or character set without tokenization. Raw UTF-8 bytes capture spelling, punctuation, emoji, and markup directly. This is useful for multilingual and noisy text scenarios where tokenizers may struggle.

JEPA pretraining learns representations by predicting clean latent embeddings from corrupted views. The teacher encoder processes clean text while the student encoder sees corrupted or incomplete versions. A predictor learns to recover the original representations.

Canonical segmentation splits the byte stream into fixed-size chunks of 32 bytes each. Segments preserve the original byte positions and indices are used consistently throughout training and inference. Missing segments are predicted from surrounding context.

The EMA teacher is updated by exponential moving average of the student parameters. This provides stable targets that improve gradually during training. The momentum increases from 0.996 to 0.9999 following a cosine schedule.

Corruption strategies include missing spans, segment dropout, foreign replacement, local reorder, and byte-level noise. Each corruption type receives a different loss weight to balance the training signal. Missing and replaced segments have the highest weights.

The local byte processor uses convolutional blocks to capture character-level patterns within each 32-byte segment. Conv1D with SwiGLU activation provides efficient local feature extraction before segment reduction by pooling.

Segment reduction combines mean pooling, attention pooling, first byte, and last byte states to create a single vector representing each segment. A linear projection maps this combined representation to the model dimension.

The cross-attention predictor generates predictions for all canonical positions by attending to the student encoder context. Target queries are learned embeddings combined with positional encodings for each canonical segment index.

Anti-collapse regularization monitors embedding variance and covariance during training. Variance regularization penalizes dimensions with low standard deviation. Covariance regularization reduces off-diagonal covariance between embedding dimensions.
